title: Castle in the Air - Chapter Two: In which Abdullah is mistaken for a young lady (Fragment 1)
text:
Castle in the Air

Chapter Two

I N WHICH A BDULLAH IS MISTAKEN FOR A YOUNG LADY

He woke to find himself lying on a bank, with the carpet still underneath him, in a garden more beautiful than any he had imagined.

Abdullah was convinced that this was a dream. Here was the garden he had been trying to imagine when the stranger so rudely interrupted him. Here the moon was nearly full and riding high above, casting light as white as paint on a hundred small fragrant flowers in the grass around him. Round yellow lamps hung in the trees, dispelling the dense black shadows from the moon. Abdullah thought this was a very pleasing idea. By the two lights, white and yellow, he could see an arcade of creepers supported on elegant pillars, beyond the lawn where he lay, and from somewhere behind that, hidden water was quietly trickling.

It was so cool and so heavenlike that Abdullah got up and went in search of the hidden water, wandering down the arcade, where starry blooms brushed his face, all white and hushed in the moonlight, and bell-like flowers breathed out the headiest and gentlest of scents. As one does in dreams, Abdullah fingered a great waxy lily here and detoured deliciously there into a dell of pale roses. He had never before had a dream that was anything like so beautiful.

The water, when he found it beyond some big fernlike bushes dripping dew, was a simple marble fountain in another lawn, lit by strings of lamps in the bushes, which made the rippling water into a marvel of gold and silver crescents. Abdullah wandered toward it raptly.

There was only one thing needed to complete his rapture, and as in all the best dreams, it was there. An extremely lovely girl came across the lawn to meet him, treading softly on the damp grass with bare feet. The gauzy garments floating around her showed her to be slender, but not thin, just like the princess from Abdullah’s daydream. When she was near Abdullah, he saw that her face was not quite a perfect oval as the face of his dream princess should have been, nor were her huge dark eyes at all misty. In fact, they examined his face keenly, with evident interest. Abdullah hastily adjusted his dream, for she was certainly very beautiful. And when she spoke, her voice was all he could have desired, being light and merry as the water in the fountain and the voice of a very definite person, too.

“Are you a new kind of servant?” she said.

People always did ask strange things in dreams, Abdullah thought. “No, masterpiece of my imagination,” he said. “Know that I am really the long-lost son of a distant prince.”

“Oh,” she said. “Then that may make a difference. Does that mean you’re a different kind of woman from me?”

Abdullah stared at the girl of his dreams in some perplexity. “ I’m not a woman!” he said.

meta.author: Diana Wynne Jones

vocabulary.definition: A covered passage with arches along one or both sides.
vocabulary.example: He wandered down the arcade, where starry blooms brushed his face.

vocabulary.definition: Shaped or sounding like a bell.
vocabulary.example: Bell-like flowers breathed out the headiest and gentlest of scents.

vocabulary.definition: To emit or give off (a scent or breath).
vocabulary.example: Bell-like flowers breathed out the headiest and gentlest of scents.

vocabulary.definition: Plants that grow along the ground or up surfaces, attaching themselves as they grow.
vocabulary.example: An arcade of creepers supported on elegant pillars.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that is delightful or highly pleasing to the senses.
vocabulary.example: He detoured deliciously there into a dell of pale roses.

vocabulary.definition: A small secluded valley or hollow.
vocabulary.example: He detoured deliciously there into a dell of pale roses.

vocabulary.definition: Closely packed together, thick.
vocabulary.example: Dispelling the dense black shadows from the moon.

vocabulary.definition: To make a detour or go out of the way.
vocabulary.example: He detoured deliciously there into a dell of pale roses.

vocabulary.definition: Graceful and stylish in appearance or manner.
vocabulary.example: An arcade of creepers supported on elegant pillars.

vocabulary.definition: Having a pleasant or sweet smell.
vocabulary.example: A hundred small fragrant flowers in the grass around him.

vocabulary.definition: Thin and translucent, like gauze fabric.
vocabulary.example: The gauzy garments floating around her showed her to be slender.

vocabulary.definition: Overpowering or intoxicating in effect, often referring to scent or atmosphere.
vocabulary.example: Bell-like flowers breathed out the headiest and gentlest of scents.

vocabulary.definition: Resembling or characteristic of heaven; divine or beautiful.
vocabulary.example: It was so cool and so heavenlike that Abdullah got up.

vocabulary.definition: The faculty or action of forming new ideas or images not present to the senses.
vocabulary.example: “No, masterpiece of my imagination,” he said.

vocabulary.definition: A type of hard, crystalline limestone often used in sculpture and architecture.
vocabulary.example: The water was a simple marble fountain in another lawn.

vocabulary.definition: Wrongly identified or understood.
vocabulary.example: I N WHICH ABDULLAH IS MISTAKEN FOR A YOUNG LADY.

vocabulary.definition: Inability to deal with or understand something; confusion.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah stared at the girl of his dreams in some perplexity.

vocabulary.definition: Tall vertical structures used to support a building or other structure.
vocabulary.example: An arcade of creepers supported on elegant pillars.

vocabulary.definition: With complete interest and attention.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah wandered toward it raptly.

vocabulary.definition: Forming small waves on the surface of water.
vocabulary.example: The rippling water made a marvel of gold and silver crescents.

vocabulary.definition: A person whom one does not know.
vocabulary.example: The stranger so rudely interrupted him.

vocabulary.definition: Held up or borne by something.
vocabulary.example: An arcade of creepers supported on elegant pillars.

vocabulary.definition: Walking on or along something.
vocabulary.example: She was treading softly on the damp grass with bare feet.

vocabulary.definition: Having the appearance or texture of wax.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah fingered a great waxy lily.

vocabulary.definition: Made by interlacing threads.
vocabulary.example: The gauzy garments floating around her showed her to be slender.
